articles.title: Getting Started with Our Knowledge Base
articles.content:
Welcome to the Knowledge Base

This internal knowledge management system is designed to help our team document, organize, and share important information about our projects, processes, and research findings.

Key Features

  • Rich text editing with support for images and files
  • Hierarchical organization through categories
  • Tag-based classification for easy searching
  • Draft and published status management
  • File attachment support

Start creating your first article to contribute to our collective knowledge!
articles.summary: An introduction to using our internal knowledge management system effectively.
articles.author: negin

articles.title: Research Methodology Framework
articles.content:
Our Research Approach

This document outlines the systematic approach we use for conducting research and documenting findings.

Research Phases

  • Problem Definition: Clearly articulate the research question
  • Literature Review: Survey existing knowledge and solutions
  • Methodology Selection: Choose appropriate research methods
  • Data Collection: Gather relevant information and evidence
  • Analysis: Process and interpret the collected data
  • Documentation: Record findings and recommendations

Each research project should follow this framework to ensure consistency and quality.
articles.summary: A systematic framework for conducting and documenting research projects.
articles.author: negin

articles.title: Team Onboarding Checklist
articles.content:
New Team Member Onboarding

Welcome to the team! This checklist will help ensure you have everything needed to get started.

First Week

  • Set up development environment
  • Review team documentation in this knowledge base
  • Attend team meetings and introductions
  • Complete initial training modules

First Month

  • Shadow experienced team members
  • Contribute to your first project
  • Add your first article to the knowledge base
  • Complete onboarding feedback survey
articles.summary: Essential steps and resources for new team members joining our organization.
articles.author: negin

articles.title: Building Smart IoT Systems with Python and Rust
articles.content:
Introduction

Internet of Things (IoT) systems are transforming the way we interact with the physical world. From smart homes to industrial automation, IoT connects devices through the power of software and networks.

Why Python and Rust?

  • Python offers ease of use, rich libraries, and rapid prototyping capabilities.
  • Rust ensures safety, concurrency, and performance in embedded or edge computing.

Combining both can lead to a powerful hybrid architecture.

Example Use Case

Consider a smart greenhouse system. Python handles the data analytics and dashboard, while Rust manages low-level sensor control.

# Python Example: reading temperature data
import requests
response = requests.get('http://greenhouse.local/sensors/temp')
print(response.json())
// Rust Example: controlling the actuator
fn main() {
    println!("Turning on water pump...");
}

This combination offers the best of both worlds: developer speed and system-level reliability.
articles.summary: A practical overview of how to connect and automate devices using Python and Rust for IoT applications
articles.author: Negin
